session_management.title: Session Preparation and Running
session_management.overview: Guidelines for preparing and running effective D&D game sessions

card_accessories.title: Card Accessories
card_accessories.description: Many players and DMs find cards helpful for quick reference
benefits: Reduces need to flip through books

one_hour_guideline.title: The One-Hour Guideline
one_hour_guideline.overview: Most groups can accomplish at least three 'things' during one hour of active play
one_hour_guideline.note: Sessions usually start with out-of-game chatter as everyone settles in

things_accomplished.activity: Reach consensus on a divisive issue
Solve a tricky riddle or puzzle
Fight a more difficult combat encounter

things_accomplished.count: Most or all of an hour

one_hour_guideline.application: Use this to gauge session pacing and what you can realistically accomplish

session_preparation.title: Preparing for a Session
session_preparation.overview: Guidelines for preparation time based on available time (using published adventures)
session_preparation.general_principle: More preparation leads to smoother play, but there's a point of diminishing returns

steps.title: Focus on the Story
actions: Read or reread the adventure's introduction
Review background information
Create a bulleted list of key plot points
Ensure a coherent story unfolds

steps.title: Identify Encounters and Likelihood
actions: Determine which encounters you want to run
Assess how likely each encounter will occur
Categorize encounters: 'definite,' 'possible,' or 'unlikely'
Focus preparation on likely encounters

steps.title: Gather Maps and Prepare Definite Encounters
actions: Collect maps for definite and possible encounters
Focus remaining prep time on definite encounters
For combat: review monster tactics and stat blocks, note special rules
For social: note NPC personalities, goals, and tactics
For exploration: record clues and information, review special rules

steps.title: Connect to Player Motivations
actions: Consider how each definite encounter relates to player motivations
Think about elements to add to interest players
Example: open combat with negotiation for social players
Make encounters personally relevant

steps.title: Skim Possible Encounters
actions: Briefly review encounters flagged as possible
Prepare enough to run them if they occur

additional_steps.title: Carefully Review Possible Encounters
actions: In-depth review of each 'possible' encounter
Prepare tactics, NPCs, and key information
Be ready to run these if they come up

additional_steps.title: Create Improvisational Aids
actions: Devote remaining time to improvisation tools
Random encounter tables
NPC personality notes
Quick reference sheets
Quick descriptions for unexpected situations

additional_steps.title: Review Unlikely Encounters
actions: Skim each 'unlikely' encounter
Know the basics in case they're encountered
Don't spend extensive time here

additional_steps.title: Create Personalized Encounters
actions: Create a new encounter for one player's character
Or alter an existing encounter to relate to that character's goals
Over several sessions, do this for all players
Makes each player feel special

session_preparation.tip: Prioritize based on available time; don't over-prepare

running_a_session.title: How to Run a Session
running_a_session.overview: Step-by-step guidance for running a game session

session_recap.title: Starting with a Recap
session_recap.when: Start each game session after the first with a recap
purpose: Helps players get back into the story
Provides information to players who missed previous session

use_when: You have specific information to impart
You want the recap to be concise
You want to focus on relevant details
You want to gauge what they think is important
You want to learn what they're getting from the game
benefits: Can add missing details as needed

session_recap.tip: If players miss important details in their recap, you can interject a reminder

encounters.title: Encounters and Their Structure
encounters.overview: The bulk of a typical session consists of a series of encounters, similar to scenes in a movie
encounters.structure: Each encounter has chances for DM to describe and for characters to make choices

encounter_step_1.title: Step 1: Describe the Situation
encounter_step_1.overview: As the DM, you control what information players receive
encounter_step_1.core_principle: Tell players everything they need to know within the rules and limits of character knowledge and senses

guidance.description: Text in boxes is meant to be read aloud when characters first arrive
guidance.purpose: Describes locations so players know what's happening and options available

guidance.description: Initial description should focus on what characters can perceive
guidance.note: Don't reveal every detail at once

guidance.attention_span: Most players lose focus after about three sentences
guidance.approach: Give initial overview, then provide details as characters search and examine

guidance.description: As characters search rooms, open drawers, examine things, give more details

encounter_step_2.title: Step 2: Let the Players Talk
encounter_step_2.overview: After describing the situation, ask what the characters want to do
process: Ask what players' characters want to do
Identify how to resolve their actions
Ask for more information if needed

individual_actions.example: One searches a chest while another examines a bookshelf
individual_actions.handling: Outside combat, no initiative turns; give each player a chance to act

outside_combat: Characters don't need to take turns
Give each player a chance to describe what their character does
in_combat: Everyone takes turns in Initiative order
One action at a time following action economy

encounter_step_3.title: Step 3: Describe What Happens
encounter_step_3.overview: After players describe their actions, resolve them guided by rules and preparation

resolution_methods.example: Adventurer crosses empty room and opens door
resolution_methods.approach: Simply describe what happens (reference map/notes if needed)
Usually call for D20 Test (often ability check)
examples_shown: Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) to pick lock

approach: Roleplay based on creature attitude (Friendly, Indifferent, Hostile)
Improvise based on creature knowledge and motivations
Bring creatures to life in description

resolution_methods.approach: Keep action economy rules in mind when adjudicating

approach: Use attack rolls and saving throws
See 'Running Combat' section for detailed advice
Usually let player tell you spell effects and resolution
If questions arise, read spell text
Use general spellcasting rules as backup

resolution_methods.principle: Class features, spells, magic items, abilities override general rules
resolution_methods.example: General rule: melee attacks use Strength. Exception: class feature allows Charisma for melee attacks. Exception wins.

narration_guidance: Clearly communicate what's happening using game language
Make results feel satisfying and dramatic
encounter_step_3.cycle: Describing results often leads to another decision point, returning to Step 1

passing_time.title: Passing Time Between Encounters
passing_time.overview: The game has rhythm with periods of action interspersed with lulls
passing_time.principle: Think of how movies show time passing between scenes

guidance.action: Gloss over with quick narrative summary
Tell players it passes uneventfully and move on
Gloss over unless players specifically enjoy such descriptions

passing_time.core_approach: Return to action as quickly as possible
player_discussions: You don't need to be involved unless they have questions
Let them discuss; you can step back
passing_time.dm_responsibility: Learn to recognize when you can take a break as DM and resume when ready

taking_breaks.title: Taking Breaks During Play
when: When you need time to think
purpose: Give your brain moments to refocus
taking_breaks.benefit: It's OK to leave players in suspense while you figure out consequences of their actions

ending_a_session.title: Ending a Game Session
ending_a_session.general_guideline: Try not to end in the middle of an encounter
ending_a_session.reason: Difficult to track Initiative, round details, and other combat information between sessions
ending_a_session.exception: You can end with a cliffhanger

cliffhanger.definition: Story pauses just as something monumental happens or surprising turn of events occurs
benefits: Can bring back missing player's character

missing_players: If a player missed a session and their character left the party
Ensure there's a way to bring the character back when player returns
Cliffhanger can serve this purpose (character charges in to help)
end_of_session_routine: Allow a few minutes for everyone to discuss session events
Ask players what parts they liked
Ask what they would have liked to see more of
Record the situation at session's end
Refer back to notes when preparing next session

session_notes.title: Tracking Session Information
why_important: Helps remember details players asked about
